prompt:
Scene:
A square photographic image with intentional lens choice, coherent light, and a subject that feels observed rather than merely decorated. Build the frame around a dusk, night, or low-light frame with disciplined exposure. Keep the lighting, camera distance, and spatial organization tuned for a high-signal research image rather than a casual snapshot. The image belongs to the Underwater Bubble Light theme and should feel immediately legible as a prompt atlas theme for underwater bubble light, testing photorealism, physics-materials with gpt image 2..

Subject:
Center the study on a dusk, night, or low-light frame with disciplined exposure. Translate the theme into a concrete visual statement instead of an abstract mood board: the viewer should understand what kind of image this is, what is being tested, and why the composition is difficult. Use the slug idea of underwater bubble light as a literal guide for subject matter, materials, and staging.

Important details:
The realism should come from timing, optics, and material response: atmosphere, contrast, depth, motion, and tiny physical cues all need to agree. Preserve a disciplined square composition, clear focal hierarchy, and enough secondary information to make the image feel complete rather than underspecified. The image should look polished, novel, and exhibition-ready, with topic-specific credibility coming from the way surfaces, structure, and visual language reinforce Underwater Bubble Light. Avoid generic filler elements that do not serve the main concept.

Use case:
Underwater Bubble Light single study 5/8, testing photorealism, physics-materials through the low-light atlas variation.

Constraints:
No readable text, no watermark, no logos, preserve exact 1:1 aspect ratio, no watermark, no cheap HDR excess, no unreadable signage, no fake stock-photo posing, no compositing seams, no arbitrary lens flare.
title: Low-Light Atlas
description: Low-Light Atlas interprets Underwater Bubble Light as a self-contained image study with a distinct visual emphasis.
